title: Industry Physicist — Quantum Computing Startup
setting: An Australian quantum computing startup (e.g. in Sydney or Melbourne)
description: You work as a physicist in a quantum computing company, bridging the gap between fundamental physics and product development. Your days involve designing qubit experiments, analysing noise data, building simulation tools, and working closely with engineers to improve hardware performance. The pace is faster than academia and the problems are deeply applied.

schedule.activity: Stand-up meeting with the quantum hardware team
schedule.note: Review yesterday's qubit coherence measurements, plan today's experiments

schedule.activity: Analyse T1 and T2 coherence data from overnight dilution fridge runs
schedule.note: Looking for correlations between coherence times and fabrication parameters

schedule.activity: Design a new pulse sequence for two-qubit gate calibration
schedule.note: Optimising gate fidelity requires precise microwave pulse shaping

schedule.activity: Physics deep-dive meeting — discuss a noise mechanism with theorists
schedule.note: Identifying whether the noise is 1/f or white helps choose mitigation strategies

schedule.activity: Lunch — company-provided, eat with the cryogenics team
schedule.note: Startup culture means everyone knows everyone

schedule.activity: Write simulation code to model qubit-resonator coupling
schedule.note: Simulation guides the next hardware design iteration

schedule.activity: Review a colleague's paper draft for an upcoming conference
schedule.note: Industry labs still publish — it attracts talent and builds credibility

schedule.activity: Work with engineers on the next chip design — provide physics input
schedule.note: Electromagnetic simulations guide the resonator and qubit geometry

schedule.activity: Wrap up, push code to Git, and write up experiment notes
schedule.note: Documentation is critical when iterating fast

responsibilities: Design and execute qubit characterisation experiments
Analyse experimental data to improve hardware performance
Build simulation tools that guide hardware design
Collaborate with engineers on chip fabrication and cryogenics
Present results to investors and stakeholders
Stay current with the latest quantum computing research
Contribute to the company's intellectual property portfolio
challenges: The pressure to demonstrate commercial viability of quantum technology
Working at the bleeding edge where theory and experiment do not always agree
Balancing scientific rigour with startup speed
Communicating complex physics to non-physicist stakeholders and investors
Recruiting top talent in a competitive global market
rewards: Working on technology that could transform computing
Faster pace and more tangible impact than academia
Seeing your physics directly improve a real product
Collaborating with world-class physicists and engineers
careerPath: Physicist → Senior physicist → Principal physicist → VP of Quantum Hardware / CTO
